metadata.title: Your benchmark score may be a memorization score in disguise
metadata.topic: Separating recall from reasoning with a training-cutoff split
metadata.purpose: Historical exploits generate public post-mortems and write-ups that enter training corpora; a temporal split makes recall visible as a performance cliff exactly at the cutoff boundary.
metadata.clock: narration (Kokoro am_onyx) — master clock; conform each beat to measured audio
metadata.exclusions: EVM/Solidity specifics, vulnerability taxonomy, any single incident's narrative
metadata.visual_object: Two score bars — pre-cutoff vs post-cutoff — with a vertical cutoff line between; the gap is the "memorization premium."
metadata.hook: A model that solves 80% of historical DeFi exploits might just be reciting the incident write-ups it read during training.

beats.narration_text: The 12 post-cutoff incidents (January 2026 onward) postdate current models' training data, so the only way to solve them is from the contract code itself — not a memorized post-mortem.
beats.scaffold_note: from Key case — cold open on the concrete instance

beats.narration_text: If a model scores higher on pre-cutoff incidents than post-cutoff ones, why does the split reveal memorization rather than just difficulty?
A model that solves 80% of historical DeFi exploits might just be reciting the incident write-ups it read during training.
Historical exploits generate public post-mortems and write-ups that enter training corpora; a temporal split makes recall visible as a performance cliff exactly at the cutoff boundary.
beats.visual: Two score bars — pre-cutoff vs post-cutoff — with a vertical cutoff line between; the gap is the "memorization premium."
beats.scaffold_note: the resolving mechanism; orbits the visual object

beats.narration_text: 65% on 405 pre-2026 incidents, 35% on 12 post-January-2026 incidents; the 30-point drop estimates how many "solved" tasks were recalled, not reasoned.
Historical exploits generate public post-mortems and write-ups that enter training corpora; a temporal split makes recall visible as a performance cliff exactly at the cutoff boundary.
